title: Bodily ownership and self-location: components of bodily self-consciousness.

assets.abstract: Recent research on bodily self-consciousness has assumed that it consists of three distinct components: the experience of owning a body (body ownership); the experience of being a body with a given location within the environment (self-location); and the experience of taking a first-person, body-centered, perspective on that environment (perspective). Here we review recent neuroimaging studies suggesting that at least two of these components-body ownership and self-location-are implemented in ra

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Body illusion — multisensory integration, rubber hand illusion, body ownership as constructed percept.
